##### About me

I created Mama Bare Doula LLC out of a passion for walking alongside mothers and families during one of the most meaningful times of their lives. My philosophy is simple: every mother deserves to feel supported, cared for, and confident from pregnancy through postpartum. I believe birth is not only about bringing a baby into the world but also about nurturing and strengthening the mother. My role is to provide comfort, advocacy, and encouragement while helping families embrace their unique journey. Whether you are preparing for birth at home, in a birth center, or in the hospital, I am committed to honoring your choices and holding space for your story while offering continuous care and heartfelt support.

##### Education

Na (2022)

Birth doula dona international (2022)

##### Specialties

Birth DoulaBirth SupportBreastfeeding Assistance and SupportLabor DoulaParenting SupportPositive ThinkingPostpartum CarePostpartum Doula CarePostpartum Life

Show less

##### Service introduction

Motherhood is sacred and so is your story. I’m here to offer gentle guidance, support, and love from pregnancy to postpartum.

##### Packages

Basic package

Basic Birth Doula Package – $1,200 • Two prenatal visits to prepare for birth, discuss preferences, and practice comfort techniques • Unlimited phone, text, and email support throughout pregnancy • On-call availability beginning at 38 weeks until birth • Continuous labor and birth support at home, birth center, or hospital • One postpartum follow-up visit for processing your birth story, breastfeeding support, and newborn care guidance

$1,500.00

Get started

The cost of a doula in Tyler, TX can vary depending on the experience and services offered by the doula. On average, the cost of hiring a doula in Tyler, TX can range from $800 to $2000. It is important to research and interview multiple doulas to find one that fits your budget and meets your specific needs.

The cost of a postpartum doula in Tyler, TX can vary widely depending on the individual doula's experience, services offered, and the length of time needed. On average, postpartum doula services in Tyler, TX can range from $25 to $40 per hour. Some doulas also offer package deals for a set number of hours or days. It's best to contact individual doulas in the area to get specific pricing information.

It's important to note that doula fees can vary significantly within the Tyler area, with some highly experienced or specialized doulas charging higher rates. Additionally, some doulas offer package deals that may include prenatal visits, continuous labor support, and postpartum support, which can affect the overall cost.


When considering hiring a doula in Tyler, it's advisable to research and interview several doulas to find one who aligns with your preferences, needs, and budget. During consultations, you can discuss their specific services and pricing structure to ensure you have a clear understanding of what is included and the associated costs. This process will help you find a doula who meets your needs and fits within your budget.


## How to find a doula near me

01

### Tell us your doula needs

Answer a few quick questions regarding your due date, birth plan, location, budget etc.


02

### Match with doulas

We will search our database of 4,000+ doulas and instantly bring a list of certified doulas to you.


03

### Start messaging

Chat with your matched doulas, compare options and schedule consultations to find the right fit.


### Get matched

Share details about your doula needs ranging from birth prep, breastfeeding, baby sleep, postpartum recovery, or newborn care, and we will match you with the best local doulas.


Find your doula

### Extensive doula network

Begin matching with our network of 4,000+ doulas, easily message those who match your needs, and show interest in learning more.

Found 8 Doulas in Tyler, TX


[![Kayley hamilton](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/fl26zdgvbuajbbsx4rmd0xtr5o6m.jpeg)\\
\\
Tyler, Texas\\
\\
**Kayley hamilton**\\
\\
My birth and postpartum philosophy centers on deeply nurturing the mother as a whole—spiritually, physically, and emotionally—during one of the most transformative seasons of her life. I believe that when a mother is supported, seen, and cared for, she is better able to bond with and care for her baby. I approach each family with compassion, presence, and respect, honoring their unique journey and intuition. Physically, I provide gentle, practical support that promotes rest, recovery, and confidence. Emotionally, I create a safe, nonjudgmental space where mothers can process their experiences and feel truly heard. Spiritually, I hold space for reflection, grounding, and connection, helping mothers feel rooted and empowered as they transition into this new chapter. My goal is to ensure no mother feels alone, but instead held, supported, and strengthened in mind, body, and spirit.](https://www.bornbir.com/kayley-hamilton)

[![Stephanie Harris](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/99trlx87twmqt2zjd2i8iv3hhkhc.png)\\
\\
Big Sandy, Texas\\
\\
**Stephanie Harris**\\
\\
11 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Stephanie, the heart behind Cedar & Honey Birth Co. I’m a birth doula and licensed massage therapist with over a decade of experience supporting moms through pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum season.\\
My approach to birth is calm, steady, and deeply supportive. I believe every mom deserves to feel informed, respected, and well cared for — physically, emotionally, and spiritually — throughout her journey. I offer hands-on comfort measures, evidence-based education, advocacy in hospital settings, and continuous presence so you feel supported and confident during birth.\\
With a background in medical and therapeutic massage, I bring advanced bodywork skills into labor support — helping with comfort, relaxation, positioning, and nervous system regulation. I’m trauma-informed, intuitive, and experienced working alongside healthcare professionals, allowing me to support you confidently whether your birth follows your hopes or takes an unexpected turn.\\
Faith is an important part of who I am, and I naturally integrate prayer, encouragement, and spiritual support into my care. This is woven into the way I serve moms and families, with gentleness, reverence, and trust in God’s presence throughout birth.\\
My heart is to create a peaceful, reassuring space where you feel prepared, supported, and confident as you welcome your baby. It would be an honor to walk this journey with you.](https://www.bornbir.com/stephanie-harris)

[![Alissa January](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/uihv4o056zwsw9t6toariwcxwbd7.jpeg)\\
\\
Longview, Texas\\
\\
**Alissa January**\\
\\
2 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hey Everyone! My name is Alissa January and I am a certified birth doula in Longview, Tx. However, I serve all of East Texas! My passion is serving mamas through their pregnancy, labor, and postpartum journey! I have a lot of different packages I offer for you to choose from! Book your FREE consultation today on my website and get connected with me to discuss how I can best serve you!](https://www.bornbir.com/alissa-january)

[![Emily Barton](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/va72o44po0lf0kj3wt3d4g30mcgj.jpeg)\\
\\
Longview, Texas\\
\\
**Emily Barton**\\
\\
Hi, I’m Emily! I’m a mama of four and a birth doula serving families throughout East Texas. Motherhood and birth have always been something deeply important to me, and after having my own children, I knew I wanted to support other women through one of the most vulnerable and powerful seasons of their lives.\\
\\
I truly love what I do. My goal as a doula is to help families feel supported, informed, and cared for throughout pregnancy, labor, birth, and postpartum. I support all types of births and believe every family deserves to feel heard, respected, and empowered in their experience. Whether you’re planning an unmedicated birth, epidural, induction, cesarean, or home birth, I’m there to provide emotional support, education, comfort measures, and encouragement every step of the way.\\
\\
I strive to create a warm, judgment-free space where you can ask questions, feel safe, and know you don’t have to do this alone.](https://www.bornbir.com/emily-barton)

[![Mallory Villafuerte](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/onn3p813hyz3749moe21ss5uvdb2.jpg)\\
\\
Tyler, Texas\\
\\
**Mallory Villafuerte**\\
\\
4 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Mallory the heart behind The Crunchy-ish Doula.\\
I’ve been married to my husband for nine years, and together we’re raising three beautiful children: a curious 7 year old, a spirited 4 year old, and our sweet 2 year old. Motherhood has been one of my greatest teachers, and every birth story I carry my own and the ones I’m honored to witness has shaped the way I serve families.\\
I welcomed my first child in a hospital setting. While I’m grateful for that experience, it also opened my eyes to how unprepared and disconnected many women can feel during birth. After that birth, and through the heartache of several miscarriages, I spent a lot of time praying asking the Lord to show me my purpose and how He wanted to use my story.\\
In His perfect timing, God answered a lifelong prayer.\\
After the home birth of my second son, everything became clear. I experienced firsthand the power of education, support, and trust in the body God designed. That birth changed me and in 2021, I became a doula.\\
My calling is to walk alongside families with open hands and a steady presence, honoring both faith and informed decision making. I believe God created our bodies with intention and wisdom, and I also believe there are moments when medical intervention is necessary and life saving. My role is never to push an agenda, but to help you prepare, discern, and advocate with confidence and peace no matter where or how you give birth.\\
Birth is sacred. Mothers are strong. And no family should feel alone in this season.\\
It would be an honor to support you.](https://www.bornbir.com/mallory-villafuerte)

[![Camilla Morgan](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/9z4xwjcgkz7ankszrdcdjwwzjvtl.jpeg)\\
\\
Tyler, Texas\\
\\
**Camilla Morgan**\\
\\
5 years of experience\\

\\
Hello, I'm Cam—a passionate birth and postpartum doula on a mission to provide unwavering support to individuals and families throughout the incredible journey of childbirth and early parenthood. My own transformative experiences as a mother ignited a deep commitment to empower and nurture others during this pivotal chapter of life, leading me to embark on the fulfilling path of doula work.\\
\\
My journey as a birth doula revolves around creating positive and empowering birthing experiences. I'm dedicated to offering emotional encouragement, evidence-based information, and physical comfort to expectant parents as they navigate the intricacies of labor and delivery. Advocating for birthing individuals, I ensure they feel informed, empowered, and supported in making choices aligned with their values and preferences. Seamlessly transitioning into my role as a postpartum doula, I extend support to new parents, providing guidance on newborn care, breastfeeding, and postpartum recovery. My non-judgmental and compassionate approach fosters a secure space, allowing families to thrive during the transformative early days of parenthood.](https://www.bornbir.com/camilla-morgan)

Whether you are preparing for birth at home, in a birth center, or in the hospital, I am committed to honoring your choices and holding space for your story while offering continuous care and heartfelt support.](https://www.bornbir.com/tabitha-oogjen)

As your doula, my goal is to support and guide you throughout your pregnancy and childbirth journey. I am dedicated to helping you make informed decisions, be\\

# Reflecting on October

October was full of activities. We gathered for First Friday and Community Masses, explored meaningful themes in our Quest and Voyages meetings, and served the St. Mary Magdalene Food Pantry by preparing hygiene packets for their customers. Even though Texas hasn’t gotten the memo about the fall weather, this month concluded with fun autumn activities and learning at the Pumpkin Patch.

Last month we also held a special celebration for the feast day of St. Pope John Paul II, the beloved patron of our group.

# Find a Birth Doula Near Me in Tyler, TX

Answer a few questions, and match directly with top-rated and certified birth doulas near you in Tyler, TX - all in 30 seconds, and it's entirely free!


Get matched now

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/production/static/sipqsp5rstgzzq7sgfhr.png)

### Save time

No more endless searching online


### Save money

Compare your options and pricing


### Global movement

Both in-person and virtual support available


## How to find a doula near me

01

### Tell us your doula needs

Answer a few quick questions regarding your due date, birth plan, location, budget etc.


02

### Match with doulas

We will search our database of 4,000+ doulas and instantly bring a list of certified doulas to you.


03

### Start messaging

Chat with your matched doulas, compare options and schedule consultations to find the right fit.


### Get matched

Share details about your doula needs ranging from birth prep, breastfeeding, baby sleep, postpartum recovery, or newborn care, and we will match you with the best local doulas.


Find your doula

![Match with providers](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto,w_490/production/static/agx03khe9ibcmq3fsuzz.png)

### Extensive doula network

Begin matching with our network of 4,000+ doulas, easily message those who match your needs, and show interest in learning more.


Match with a doula

Get started now

Hi, I’m Stephanie, the heart behind Cedar & Honey Birth Co. I’m a birth doula and licensed massage therapist with over a decade of experience supporting moms through pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum season.\\
My approach to birth is calm, steady, and deeply supportive. I believe every mom deserves to feel informed, respected, and well cared for — physically, emotionally, and spiritually — throughout her journey. I offer hands-on comfort measures, evidence-based education, advocacy in hospital settings, and continuous presence so you feel supported and confident during birth.\\
With a background in medical and therapeutic massage, I bring advanced bodywork skills into labor support — helping with comfort, relaxation, positioning, and nervous system regulation. I’m trauma-informed, intuitive, and experienced working alongside healthcare professionals, allowing me to support you confidently whether your birth follows your hopes or takes an unexpected turn.\\
Faith is an important part of who I am, and I naturally integrate prayer, encouragement, and spiritual support into my care. This is woven into the way I serve moms and families, with gentleness, reverence, and trust in God’s presence throughout birth.\\
My heart is to create a peaceful, reassuring space where you feel prepared, supported, and confident as you welcome your baby. It would be an honor to walk this journey with you.](https://www.bornbir.com/stephanie-harris)

Book your FREE consultation today on my website and get connected with me to discuss how I can best serve you!](https://www.bornbir.com/alissa-january)

[![Emily Barton](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/va72o44po0lf0kj3wt3d4g30mcgj.jpeg)\\

Longview, Texas\\

**Emily Barton**\\

Hi, I’m Emily! I’m a mama of four and a birth doula serving families throughout East Texas. Motherhood and birth have always been something deeply important to me, and after having my own children, I knew I wanted to support other women through one of the most vulnerable and powerful seasons of their lives.\\
\\
I truly love what I do. My goal as a doula is to help families feel supported, informed, and cared for throughout pregnancy, labor, birth, and postpartum. I support all types of births and believe every family deserves to feel heard, respected, and empowered in their experience. Whether you’re planning an unmedicated birth, epidural, induction, cesarean, or home birth, I’m there to provide emotional support, education, comfort measures, and encouragement every step of the way.\\
\\
I strive to create a warm, judgment-free space where you can ask questions, feel safe, and know you don’t have to do this alone.](https://www.bornbir.com/emily-barton)

I created Mama Bare Doula LLC out of a passion for walking alongside mothers and families during one of the most meaningful times of their lives. My philosophy is simple: every mother deserves to feel supported, cared for, and confident from pregnancy through postpartum. I believe birth is not only about bringing a baby into the world but also about nurturing and strengthening the mother. My role is to provide comfort, advocacy, and encouragement while helping families embrace their unique journey. Whether you are preparing for birth at home, in a birth center, or in the hospital, I am committed to honoring your choices and holding space for your story while offering continuous care and heartfelt support.](https://www.bornbir.com/tabitha-oogjen)

[![Camilla Morgan](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/9z4xwjcgkz7ankszrdcdjwwzjvtl.jpeg)\\

Hello, I'm Cam—a passionate birth and postpartum doula on a mission to provide unwavering support to individuals and families throughout the incredible journey of childbirth and early parenthood. My own transformative experiences as a mother ignited a deep commitment to empower and nurture others during this pivotal chapter of life, leading me to embark on the fulfilling path of doula work.\\
\\
My journey as a birth doula revolves around creating positive and empowering birthing experiences. I'm dedicated to offering emotional encouragement, evidence-based information, and physical comfort to expectant parents as they navigate the intricacies of labor and delivery. Advocating for birthing individuals, I ensure they feel informed, empowered, and supported in making choices aligned with their values and preferences. Seamlessly transitioning into my role as a postpartum doula, I extend support to new parents, providing guidance on newborn care, breastfeeding, and postpartum recovery. My non-judgmental and compassionate approach fosters a secure space, allowing families to thrive during the transformative early days of parenthood.](https://www.bornbir.com/camilla-morgan)

[![Mallory Villafuerte](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/onn3p813hyz3749moe21ss5uvdb2.jpg)\\

Tyler, Texas\\

Hi, I’m Mallory the heart behind The Crunchy-ish Doula.\\
I’ve been married to my husband for nine years, and together we’re raising three beautiful children: a curious 7 year old, a spirited 4 year old, and our sweet 2 year old. Motherhood has been one of my greatest teachers, and every birth story I carry my own and the ones I’m honored to witness has shaped the way I serve families.\\
I welcomed my first child in a hospital setting. While I’m grateful for that experience, it also opened my eyes to how unprepared and disconnected many women can feel during birth. After that birth, and through the heartache of several miscarriages, I spent a lot of time praying asking the Lord to show me my purpose and how He wanted to use my story.\\
In His perfect timing, God answered a lifelong prayer.\\
After the home birth of my second son, everything became clear. I experienced firsthand the power of education, support, and trust in the body God designed. That birth changed me and in 2021, I became a doula.\\
My calling is to walk alongside families with open hands and a steady presence, honoring both faith and informed decision making. I believe God created our bodies with intention and wisdom, and I also believe there are moments when medical intervention is necessary and life saving. My role is never to push an agenda, but to help you prepare, discern, and advocate with confidence and peace no matter where or how you give birth.\\
Birth is sacred. Mothers are strong. And no family should feel alone in this season.\\
It would be an honor to support you.](https://www.bornbir.com/mallory-villafuerte)

## Doula Services FAQs

### What is a doula and why should you hire one?

A doula is a trained support specialist who provides continuous physical, emotional, and informational support to the mother before, during, and after childbirth. 98.6% of births in the U.S. take place in hospitals, and nearly all of them require some kind of medical intervention. However, doula-supported deliveries are linked to shorter labor, decreased need for medications, fewer c-sections, higher rates of breastfeeding initiation, less anxiety, and less postpartum depression.


### Can you pay for a doula with HSA or FSA Funds in Tyler, TX?

To use HSA or FSA funds for doula services, they generally needed to be considered medically necessary. You might need a doctor's note or prescription stating that doula services are necessary for a specific medical condition. Keep detailed records of payments and any supporting documentation, such as a doctor's note or prescription, to substantiate that the expense was medically necessary.


### How much does a birth doula cost in Tyler, TX?

The cost of a doula in Tyler, TX can vary depending on the experience and services offered by the doula. On average, the cost of hiring a doula in Tyler, TX can range from $800 to $2000. It is important to research and interview multiple doulas to find one that fits your budget and meets your specific needs.


### Are doulas covered by insurance in Tyler, TX?

Whether insurance covers the cost of a doula in Tyler, TX, depends on your specific insurance plan. Some insurance providers may offer partial or full coverage for doula services, especially if they are considered part of maternity care. It's best to contact your insurance company directly to inquire about coverage and ask if they require specific documentation or a certified doula. Additionally, some doulas may offer payment plans or work with Health Savings Accounts (HSAs) or Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs) to make their services more affordable.


### What types of doulas are for hire in Tyler, TX?

In Tyler, TX, you can find various types of doulas for hire, including birth doulas, postpartum doulas, and sometimes specialized roles such as antepartum doulas and bereavement doulas. Birth doulas provide emotional, physical, and informational support during labor and delivery, helping parents navigate the birthing process. Postpartum doulas focus on assisting families after birth, offering support with newborn care, breastfeeding, emotional recovery, and household tasks. Antepartum doulas support individuals with high-risk pregnancies or those needing extra care during pregnancy, while bereavement doulas offer compassionate support to families experiencing pregnancy or infant loss. Availability may vary, so it’s recommended to connect with local doula networks or organizations for specific services.


### When should I hire a doula in pregnancy?

You can start researching and contacting doulas as early as your first trimester. This allows you ample time to find a doula who aligns with your birth plan and build a rapport with them. Many people choose to hire a doula during their second trimester, around weeks 12 to 27. By this point, you likely have a clearer understanding of your birth preferences and can engage a doula who can provide support tailored to your needs. If you’re in your third trimester or even approaching your due date, it’s not too late to hire a doula. Some individuals make this decision later in pregnancy due to various reasons, and doulas can still offer valuable support and information during this time. The availability of doulas in your area may also influence when you hire one. In some regions, experienced doulas may have limited availability, so it’s wise to start your search early to secure the support you desire.


### What's the difference between a midwife and a doula?

A midwife is a trained health professional who helps women during labor, delivery, and after the birth of their babies. Midwives can provide gynecological examinations, prenatal care, and postpartum support. They are qualified to deliver babies and are trained to handle certain complications during childbirth. A doula, on the other hand, is a non-medical professional who provides emotional, physical, and educational support to a mother who is expecting, is experiencing labor, or has recently given birth. The doula's role is to help women have a safe, memorable, and empowering birthing experience. They are known for their continuous support, beginning during pregnancy and lasting through the postpartum period. While midwives have a broad scope of practice that is clinical in nature, doulas specialize in the non-clinical aspects of care. They might be preferred by mothers who are looking for continuous bedside support that is not typically provided by the medical staff. Doulas are known for their advocacy for the mother's wishes during childbirth, which can be especially valuable in hospital settings where the mother may desire a birth plan that deviates from standard procedures.


### How much does it cost to use Bornbir?

Bornbir is entirely free for new and expecting parents to use. To begin, simply tell our community of doulas what you need in your job posting and let the right providers come to you. You can then engage in direct conversations with top-rated providers to learn more and make informed decisions. Our goal is to facilitate a seamless and accessible experience for you as you embark on this transformative journey. Get started [here](https://www.bornbir.com/onboarding/intro_modal/new?mode=broadcast).

# Find a Birth Doula Near Me in Longview, TX

Answer a few questions, and match directly with top-rated and certified birth doulas near you in Longview, TX - all in 30 seconds, and it's entirely free!


Get matched now

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/production/static/sipqsp5rstgzzq7sgfhr.png)

### Save time

No more endless searching online


### Save money

Compare your options and pricing


### Global movement

Both in-person and virtual support available


## How to find a doula near me

01

### Tell us your doula needs

Answer a few quick questions regarding your due date, birth plan, location, budget etc.


02

### Match with doulas

We will search our database of 4,000+ doulas and instantly bring a list of certified doulas to you.


03

### Start messaging

Chat with your matched doulas, compare options and schedule consultations to find the right fit.


### Get matched

Share details about your doula needs ranging from birth prep, breastfeeding, baby sleep, postpartum recovery, or newborn care, and we will match you with the best local doulas.


Find your doula

![Match with providers](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto,w_490/production/static/agx03khe9ibcmq3fsuzz.png)

### Extensive doula network

Begin matching with our network of 4,000+ doulas, easily message those who match your needs, and show interest in learning more.


Match with a doula

Hey Everyone! My name is Alissa January and I am a certified birth doula in Longview, Tx. However, I serve all of East Texas! My passion is serving mamas through their pregnancy, labor, and postpartum journey! I have a lot of different packages I offer for you to choose from! Book your FREE consultation today on my website and get connected with me to discuss how I can best serve you!](https://www.bornbir.com/alissa-january)

[![Emily Barton](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/va72o44po0lf0kj3wt3d4g30mcgj.jpeg)\\

Longview, Texas\\

**Emily Barton**\\
\\
Hi, I’m Emily! I’m a mama of four and a birth doula serving families throughout East Texas. Motherhood and birth have always been something deeply important to me, and after having my own children, I knew I wanted to support other women through one of the most vulnerable and powerful seasons of their lives.\\
\\
I truly love what I do. My goal as a doula is to help families feel supported, informed, and cared for throughout pregnancy, labor, birth, and postpartum. I support all types of births and believe every family deserves to feel heard, respected, and empowered in their experience. Whether you’re planning an unmedicated birth, epidural, induction, cesarean, or home birth, I’m there to provide emotional support, education, comfort measures, and encouragement every step of the way.\\
\\
I strive to create a warm, judgment-free space where you can ask questions, feel safe, and know you don’t have to do this alone.](https://www.bornbir.com/emily-barton)

[![Stephanie Harris](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/99trlx87twmqt2zjd2i8iv3hhkhc.png)\\

Big Sandy, Texas\\

**Stephanie Harris**\\

11 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Stephanie, the heart behind Cedar & Honey Birth Co. I’m a birth doula and licensed massage therapist with over a decade of experience supporting moms through pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum season.\\
My approach to birth is calm, steady, and deeply supportive. I believe every mom deserves to feel informed, respected, and well cared for — physically, emotionally, and spiritually — throughout her journey. I offer hands-on comfort measures, evidence-based education, advocacy in hospital settings, and continuous presence so you feel supported and confident during birth.\\
With a background in medical and therapeutic massage, I bring advanced bodywork skills into labor support — helping with comfort, relaxation, positioning, and nervous system regulation. I’m trauma-informed, intuitive, and experienced working alongside healthcare professionals, allowing me to support you confidently whether your birth follows your hopes or takes an unexpected turn.\\
Faith is an important part of who I am, and I naturally integrate prayer, encouragement, and spiritual support into my care. This is woven into the way I serve moms and families, with gentleness, reverence, and trust in God’s presence throughout birth.\\
My heart is to create a peaceful, reassuring space where you feel prepared, supported, and confident as you welcome your baby. It would be an honor to walk this journey with you.](https://www.bornbir.com/stephanie-harris)

[![Tabitha Smith](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/development/gxwwdt2g46rr8vhfzfens2aapvnk.jpg)\\

Hey y’all, I am Tabitha your Louisiana doula. I am a mom of 4 wild, wacky and wonderful kids. I am a certified birth and postpartum doula and training to become a placenta encapsulation specialist.\\
\\
Growing up I wanted to be a labor and delivery nurse. When I became an adult I realized the medical field was not for me. I worked at a daycare in the infant room for 2 years. After that I worked as a chiropractic assistant for 2 years. My favorite part of the job was working with pregnant women and infants. \\
\\
It wasn’t until after I was done having babies that I discovered just what a doula does. I was immediately convinced that I found my calling. I have always loved all things birth and motherhood. I knew this was the journey I was finally meant to be on. \\
\\
“What is done in love is well done.”\\
-Vincent Van Gogh\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\

Tabitha is a wonderful doula that provides the best experience. You can see the love she pours into her clients. She comes highly recommended.\\

\- Brittany G.](https://www.bornbir.com/tabitha-smith)

[![Mallory Villafuerte](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/onn3p813hyz3749moe21ss5uvdb2.jpg)\\

Tyler, Texas\\

**Mallory Villafuerte**\\

4 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Mallory the heart behind The Crunchy-ish Doula.\\
I’ve been married to my husband for nine years, and together we’re raising three beautiful children: a curious 7 year old, a spirited 4 year old, and our sweet 2 year old. Motherhood has been one of my greatest teachers, and every birth story I carry my own and the ones I’m honored to witness has shaped the way I serve families.\\
I welcomed my first child in a hospital setting. While I’m grateful for that experience, it also opened my eyes to how unprepared and disconnected many women can feel during birth. After that birth, and through the heartache of several miscarriages, I spent a lot of time praying asking the Lord to show me my purpose and how He wanted to use my story.\\
In His perfect timing, God answered a lifelong prayer.\\
After the home birth of my second son, everything became clear. I experienced firsthand the power of education, support, and trust in the body God designed. That birth changed me and in 2021, I became a doula.\\
My calling is to walk alongside families with open hands and a steady presence, honoring both faith and informed decision making. I believe God created our bodies with intention and wisdom, and I also believe there are moments when medical intervention is necessary and life saving. My role is never to push an agenda, but to help you prepare, discern, and advocate with confidence and peace no matter where or how you give birth.\\
Birth is sacred. Mothers are strong. And no family should feel alone in this season.\\
It would be an honor to support you.](https://www.bornbir.com/mallory-villafuerte)

## Doula Services FAQs

### What is a doula and why should you hire one?

A doula is a trained support specialist who provides continuous physical, emotional, and informational support to the mother before, during, and after childbirth. 98.6% of births in the U.S. take place in hospitals, and nearly all of them require some kind of medical intervention. However, doula-supported deliveries are linked to shorter labor, decreased need for medications, fewer c-sections, higher rates of breastfeeding initiation, less anxiety, and less postpartum depression.


### Can you pay for a doula with HSA or FSA Funds in Longview, TX?

To use HSA or FSA funds for doula services, they generally needed to be considered medically necessary. You might need a doctor's note or prescription stating that doula services are necessary for a specific medical condition. Keep detailed records of payments and any supporting documentation, such as a doctor's note or prescription, to substantiate that the expense was medically necessary.


### How much does a birth doula cost in Longview, TX?

The cost of a doula in Longview, TX can vary depending on the individual doula and the services they offer. On average, the cost of a doula in Longview, TX can range from $800 to $2000 for their services. This cost may include prenatal visits, continuous labor support, and postpartum support. It's best to contact specific doulas in the area to get an accurate price for their services.


### Are doulas covered by insurance in Longview, TX?

Whether insurance covers the cost of a doula in Longview, TX, depends on your specific insurance plan and provider. Some insurance companies offer partial or full reimbursement for doula services, especially if they are deemed medically beneficial. However, coverage is not guaranteed and varies widely. It's best to contact your insurance provider directly to ask about doula coverage, required documentation, and reimbursement processes. Additionally, some doulas may offer payment plans or sliding scale fees if insurance won't cover the cost.


### What types of doulas are for hire in Longview, TX?

In Longview, TX, you can find various types of doulas for hire, including birth doulas, postpartum doulas, and sometimes specialized professionals like bereavement doulas or fertility doulas. Birth doulas provide physical, emotional, and informational support during pregnancy, labor, and delivery. Postpartum doulas assist families after the baby is born, offering help with newborn care, emotional support, and household tasks. Some doulas may also specialize in areas like lactation support or childbirth education. Availability may vary, so it’s best to research local options or contact doula networks in the area.


### When should I hire a doula in pregnancy?

You can start researching and contacting doulas as early as your first trimester. This allows you ample time to find a doula who aligns with your birth plan and build a rapport with them. Many people choose to hire a doula during their second trimester, around weeks 12 to 27. By this point, you likely have a clearer understanding of your birth preferences and can engage a doula who can provide support tailored to your needs. If you’re in your third trimester or even approaching your due date, it’s not too late to hire a doula. Some individuals make this decision later in pregnancy due to various reasons, and doulas can still offer valuable support and information during this time. The availability of doulas in your area may also influence when you hire one. In some regions, experienced doulas may have limited availability, so it’s wise to start your search early to secure the support you desire.


### What's the difference between a midwife and a doula?

A midwife is a trained health professional who helps women during labor, delivery, and after the birth of their babies. Midwives can provide gynecological examinations, prenatal care, and postpartum support. They are qualified to deliver babies and are trained to handle certain complications during childbirth. A doula, on the other hand, is a non-medical professional who provides emotional, physical, and educational support to a mother who is expecting, is experiencing labor, or has recently given birth. The doula's role is to help women have a safe, memorable, and empowering birthing experience. They are known for their continuous support, beginning during pregnancy and lasting through the postpartum period. While midwives have a broad scope of practice that is clinical in nature, doulas specialize in the non-clinical aspects of care. They might be preferred by mothers who are looking for continuous bedside support that is not typically provided by the medical staff. Doulas are known for their advocacy for the mother's wishes during childbirth, which can be especially valuable in hospital settings where the mother may desire a birth plan that deviates from standard procedures.


### How much does it cost to use Bornbir?

Bornbir is entirely free for new and expecting parents to use. To begin, simply tell our community of doulas what you need in your job posting and let the right providers come to you. You can then engage in direct conversations with top-rated providers to learn more and make informed decisions. Our goal is to facilitate a seamless and accessible experience for you as you embark on this transformative journey. Get started [here](https://www.bornbir.com/onboarding/intro_modal/new?mode=broadcast).

Motherhood has been one of my greatest teachers, and every birth story I carry my own and the ones I’m honored to witness has shaped the way I serve families.\\
I welcomed my first child in a hospital setting. While I’m grateful for that experience, it also opened my eyes to how unprepared and disconnected many women can feel during birth. After that birth, and through the heartache of several miscarriages, I spent a lot of time praying asking the Lord to show me my purpose and how He wanted to use my story.\\
In His perfect timing, God answered a lifelong prayer.\\
After the home birth of my second son, everything became clear. I experienced firsthand the power of education, support, and trust in the body God designed. That birth changed me and in 2021, I became a doula.\\
My calling is to walk alongside families with open hands and a steady presence, honoring both faith and informed decision making. I believe God created our bodies with intention and wisdom, and I also believe there are moments when medical intervention is necessary and life saving. My role is never to push an agenda, but to help you prepare, discern, and advocate with confidence and peace no matter where or how you give birth.\\
Birth is sacred. Mothers are strong. And no family should feel alone in this season.\\
It would be an honor to support you.](https://www.bornbir.com/mallory-villafuerte)

## Doula Services FAQs

### What is a doula and why should you hire one?

A doula is a trained support specialist who provides continuous physical, emotional, and informational support to the mother before, during, and after childbirth. 98.6% of births in the U.S. take place in hospitals, and nearly all of them require some kind of medical intervention. However, doula-supported deliveries are linked to shorter labor, decreased need for medications, fewer c-sections, higher rates of breastfeeding initiation, less anxiety, and less postpartum depression.


### Can you pay for a doula with HSA or FSA Funds in Bossier City, LA?

To use HSA or FSA funds for doula services, they generally needed to be considered medically necessary. You might need a doctor's note or prescription stating that doula services are necessary for a specific medical condition. Keep detailed records of payments and any supporting documentation, such as a doctor's note or prescription, to substantiate that the expense was medically necessary.


### How much does a doula cost in Bossier City, LA?

The cost of a doula in Bossier City, LA can vary depending on the experience and services provided by the doula. On average, the cost of a doula in Bossier City, LA ranges from $800 to $1500 for their services. It is important to contact individual doulas in the area to get an accurate quote for their services.


### Are doulas covered by insurance in Bossier City, LA?

Insurance coverage for a doula in Bossier City, LA, depends on your specific insurance plan and provider. Some insurance companies may offer partial or full reimbursement for doula services, especially if the services are deemed medically necessary or if they are part of a broader maternity care package. However, many plans do not traditionally cover doulas, as they are considered non-medical support. It's best to contact your insurance provider directly to inquire about coverage or reimbursement options. Additionally, some doulas may offer payment plans or work with programs designed to increase access to their services.


### What types of doulas are for hire in Bossier City, LA?

In Bossier City, LA, you can typically find various types of doulas available for hire, including birth doulas, postpartum doulas, and sometimes specialized doulas such as antepartum doulas or bereavement doulas. Birth doulas provide emotional, physical, and informational support during labor and delivery, helping to ensure a positive birth experience. Postpartum doulas assist families after childbirth, offering guidance with newborn care, emotional support, and help with household tasks to ease the transition. Antepartum doulas focus on supporting individuals experiencing high-risk pregnancies or complications before birth, while bereavement doulas provide care for families coping with pregnancy or infant loss. Availability may vary, so it’s best to research local providers or networks for specific services.


### When should I hire a doula in pregnancy?

You can start researching and contacting doulas as early as your first trimester. This allows you ample time to find a doula who aligns with your birth plan and build a rapport with them. Many people choose to hire a doula during their second trimester, around weeks 12 to 27. By this point, you likely have a clearer understanding of your birth preferences and can engage a doula who can provide support tailored to your needs. If you’re in your third trimester or even approaching your due date, it’s not too late to hire a doula. Some individuals make this decision later in pregnancy due to various reasons, and doulas can still offer valuable support and information during this time. The availability of doulas in your area may also influence when you hire one. In some regions, experienced doulas may have limited availability, so it’s wise to start your search early to secure the support you desire.


### What's the difference between a midwife and a doula?

A midwife is a trained health professional who helps women during labor, delivery, and after the birth of their babies. Midwives can provide gynecological examinations, prenatal care, and postpartum support. They are qualified to deliver babies and are trained to handle certain complications during childbirth. A doula, on the other hand, is a non-medical professional who provides emotional, physical, and educational support to a mother who is expecting, is experiencing labor, or has recently given birth. The doula's role is to help women have a safe, memorable, and empowering birthing experience. They are known for their continuous support, beginning during pregnancy and lasting through the postpartum period. While midwives have a broad scope of practice that is clinical in nature, doulas specialize in the non-clinical aspects of care. They might be preferred by mothers who are looking for continuous bedside support that is not typically provided by the medical staff. Doulas are known for their advocacy for the mother's wishes during childbirth, which can be especially valuable in hospital settings where the mother may desire a birth plan that deviates from standard procedures.


### How much does it cost to use Bornbir?

Bornbir is entirely free for new and expecting parents to use. To begin, simply tell our community of doulas what you need in your job posting and let the right providers come to you. You can then engage in direct conversations with top-rated providers to learn more and make informed decisions. Our goal is to facilitate a seamless and accessible experience for you as you embark on this transformative journey. Get started [here](https://www.bornbir.com/onboarding/intro_modal/new?mode=broadcast).

# Find a Newborn Care Specialist Near Me in Tyler, TX

Answer a few questions, and match directly with top-rated and certified newborn care specialists near you in Tyler, TX - all in 30 seconds, and it's entirely free!


Get matched now

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/production/static/sipqsp5rstgzzq7sgfhr.png)

### Save time

No more endless searching online


### Save money

Compare your options and pricing


### Global movement

Both in-person and virtual support available


## How Bornbir works

Bornbir streamlines provider selection during pregnancy and postpartum.


### Get matched

Tell us about your needs to connect with highly-rated doulas, midwives, and lactation consultants near you


Start matching

![Match with providers](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/production/static/agx03khe9ibcmq3fsuzz.png)

### Extensive provider network

Begin matching with our network of 6000+ doulas, midwives and lactation consultants, easily message those who fit your needs.


Start matching

![Discover providers near you](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/production/static/fqndvrdg7pmnmjnrl4zo.png)

### Real parent reviews

Gain insight from reviews by fellow parents to make a well-informed decision.


Start matching

![Real parent reviews](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,w_490/production/static/znrhwspd8de0g9xcbwuq.png)

Found 7 Newborn Care Specialists near Tyler, TX


[![Kayley hamilton](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/fl26zdgvbuajbbsx4rmd0xtr5o6m.jpeg)\\
\\
Tyler, Texas\\
\\
**Kayley hamilton**\\
\\
My birth and postpartum philosophy centers on deeply nurturing the mother as a whole—spiritually, physically, and emotionally—during one of the most transformative seasons of her life. I believe that when a mother is supported, seen, and cared for, she is better able to bond with and care for her baby. I approach each family with compassion, presence, and respect, honoring their unique journey and intuition. Physically, I provide gentle, practical support that promotes rest, recovery, and confidence. Emotionally, I create a safe, nonjudgmental space where mothers can process their experiences and feel truly heard. Spiritually, I hold space for reflection, grounding, and connection, helping mothers feel rooted and empowered as they transition into this new chapter. My goal is to ensure no mother feels alone, but instead held, supported, and strengthened in mind, body, and spirit.](https://www.bornbir.com/kayley-hamilton)

[![Leah Condel](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/ppmekgixfmydtldl3xxfwqezdlwg.jpeg)\\
\\
Lindale, Texas\\
\\
**Leah Condel**\\
\\
10 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Postpartum doula in Tyler, TX — offering compassionate, hands-on support for families through pregnancy, birth, newborn care, and the fourth trimester. Here to help you feel calm, confident, and cared for every step of the way. 🤍](https://www.bornbir.com/leah-condel)

[![Stephanie Harris](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/99trlx87twmqt2zjd2i8iv3hhkhc.png)\\
\\
Big Sandy, Texas\\
\\
**Stephanie Harris**\\
\\
11 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Stephanie, the heart behind Cedar & Honey Birth Co. I’m a birth doula and licensed massage therapist with over a decade of experience supporting moms through pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum season.\\
My approach to birth is calm, steady, and deeply supportive. I believe every mom deserves to feel informed, respected, and well cared for — physically, emotionally, and spiritually — throughout her journey. I offer hands-on comfort measures, evidence-based education, advocacy in hospital settings, and continuous presence so you feel supported and confident during birth.\\
With a background in medical and therapeutic massage, I bring advanced bodywork skills into labor support — helping with comfort, relaxation, positioning, and nervous system regulation. I’m trauma-informed, intuitive, and experienced working alongside healthcare professionals, allowing me to support you confidently whether your birth follows your hopes or takes an unexpected turn.\\
Faith is an important part of who I am, and I naturally integrate prayer, encouragement, and spiritual support into my care. This is woven into the way I serve moms and families, with gentleness, reverence, and trust in God’s presence throughout birth.\\
My heart is to create a peaceful, reassuring space where you feel prepared, supported, and confident as you welcome your baby. It would be an honor to walk this journey with you.](https://www.bornbir.com/stephanie-harris)

[![Mallory Villafuerte](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/onn3p813hyz3749moe21ss5uvdb2.jpg)\\
\\
Tyler, Texas\\
\\
**Mallory Villafuerte**\\
\\
4 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Mallory the heart behind The Crunchy-ish Doula.\\
I’ve been married to my husband for nine years, and together we’re raising three beautiful children: a curious 7 year old, a spirited 4 year old, and our sweet 2 year old. Motherhood has been one of my greatest teachers, and every birth story I carry my own and the ones I’m honored to witness has shaped the way I serve families.\\
I welcomed my first child in a hospital setting. While I’m grateful for that experience, it also opened my eyes to how unprepared and disconnected many women can feel during birth. After that birth, and through the heartache of several miscarriages, I spent a lot of time praying asking the Lord to show me my purpose and how He wanted to use my story.\\
In His perfect timing, God answered a lifelong prayer.\\
After the home birth of my second son, everything became clear. I experienced firsthand the power of education, support, and trust in the body God designed. That birth changed me and in 2021, I became a doula.\\
My calling is to walk alongside families with open hands and a steady presence, honoring both faith and informed decision making. I believe God created our bodies with intention and wisdom, and I also believe there are moments when medical intervention is necessary and life saving. My role is never to push an agenda, but to help you prepare, discern, and advocate with confidence and peace no matter where or how you give birth.\\
Birth is sacred. Mothers are strong. And no family should feel alone in this season.\\
It would be an honor to support you.](https://www.bornbir.com/mallory-villafuerte)

## FAQs

### How much does it cost to use Bornbir?

Bornbir is entirely free for new and expecting parents to use. To begin, simply tell our community of providers what you need in your job posting and let the right providers come to you. You can then engage in direct conversations with top-rated providers to learn more and make informed decisions. Our goal is to facilitate a seamless and accessible experience for you as you embark on this transformative journey. Get started [here](https://www.bornbir.com/onboarding/intro_modal/new?mode=broadcast).



### Why choose Bornbir?

Bornbir is the ideal choice for expectant and new parents seeking pregnancy or postpartum support and wanting to compare services. Our user-friendly platform enables you to search for providers, send messages, get pricing information, book appointments, and more. The best part? Bornbir is entirely free for parents!


### What does "Bornbir" mean?

Born /bɔːrn/ refers to childbirth, and “bir” /bɝː/ means birthday. Launched in August 2021, Bornbir’s mission is to create an ecosystem of support for aspiring, expectant, and new parents, to have access to the professional services that help them thrive.


### How does Bornbir protect user privacy?

We care about privacy issues deeply. Users’ personal data (e.g., name, email) will not be shared with any third parties. All in-app messages are secured. We do not sell any user data for profit.

# Find a Birth Doula Near Me in Shreveport, LA

Answer a few questions, and match directly with top-rated and certified birth doulas near you in Shreveport, LA - all in 30 seconds, and it's entirely free!


Get matched now

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto/production/static/sipqsp5rstgzzq7sgfhr.png)

### Save time

No more endless searching online


### Save money

Compare your options and pricing


### Global movement

Both in-person and virtual support available


## How to find a doula near me

01

### Tell us your doula needs

Answer a few quick questions regarding your due date, birth plan, location, budget etc.


02

### Match with doulas

We will search our database of 4,000+ doulas and instantly bring a list of certified doulas to you.


03

### Start messaging

Chat with your matched doulas, compare options and schedule consultations to find the right fit.


### Get matched

Share details about your doula needs ranging from birth prep, breastfeeding, baby sleep, postpartum recovery, or newborn care, and we will match you with the best local doulas.


Find your doula

![Match with providers](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto,w_490/production/static/agx03khe9ibcmq3fsuzz.png)

### Extensive doula network

Begin matching with our network of 4,000+ doulas, easily message those who match your needs, and show interest in learning more.


Match with a doula

![Discover providers near you](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,q_auto,w_490/production/static/fqndvrdg7pmnmjnrl4zo.png)

### Real parent reviews

Gain insight from reviews by fellow parents to make a well-informed decision.


Get started now

![Real parent reviews](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/f_auto,w_490/production/static/znrhwspd8de0g9xcbwuq.png)

Found 14 Birth Doulas near Shreveport, LA


[![Tabitha Smith](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/development/gxwwdt2g46rr8vhfzfens2aapvnk.jpg)\\
\\
Shreveport, Louisiana\\
\\
5.0\\
\\
(1 review)\\
\\
\\
**Tabitha Smith**\\
\\
4 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hey y’all, I am Tabitha your Louisiana doula. I am a mom of 4 wild, wacky and wonderful kids. I am a certified birth and postpartum doula and training to become a placenta encapsulation specialist.\\
\\
Growing up I wanted to be a labor and delivery nurse. When I became an adult I realized the medical field was not for me. I worked at a daycare in the infant room for 2 years. After that I worked as a chiropractic assistant for 2 years. My favorite part of the job was working with pregnant women and infants. \\
\\
It wasn’t until after I was done having babies that I discovered just what a doula does. I was immediately convinced that I found my calling. I have always loved all things birth and motherhood. I knew this was the journey I was finally meant to be on. \\
\\
“What is done in love is well done.”\\
-Vincent Van Gogh\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
Tabitha is a wonderful doula that provides the best experience. You can see the love she pours into her clients. She comes highly recommended.\\
\\
\- Brittany G.](https://www.bornbir.com/tabitha-smith)

[![Alissa January](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/uihv4o056zwsw9t6toariwcxwbd7.jpeg)\\
\\
Longview, Texas\\
\\
**Alissa January**\\
\\
2 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hey Everyone! My name is Alissa January and I am a certified birth doula in Longview, Tx. However, I serve all of East Texas! My passion is serving mamas through their pregnancy, labor, and postpartum journey! I have a lot of different packages I offer for you to choose from! Book your FREE consultation today on my website and get connected with me to discuss how I can best serve you!](https://www.bornbir.com/alissa-january)

[![Emily Barton](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/va72o44po0lf0kj3wt3d4g30mcgj.jpeg)\\
\\
Longview, Texas\\
\\
**Emily Barton**\\
\\
Hi, I’m Emily! I’m a mama of four and a birth doula serving families throughout East Texas. Motherhood and birth have always been something deeply important to me, and after having my own children, I knew I wanted to support other women through one of the most vulnerable and powerful seasons of their lives.\\
\\
I truly love what I do. My goal as a doula is to help families feel supported, informed, and cared for throughout pregnancy, labor, birth, and postpartum. I support all types of births and believe every family deserves to feel heard, respected, and empowered in their experience. Whether you’re planning an unmedicated birth, epidural, induction, cesarean, or home birth, I’m there to provide emotional support, education, comfort measures, and encouragement every step of the way.\\
\\
I strive to create a warm, judgment-free space where you can ask questions, feel safe, and know you don’t have to do this alone.](https://www.bornbir.com/emily-barton)

[![Moon Yirah](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/ayt3q5gzv472wundte285zj23g76.jpg)\\
\\
Texarkana, Texas\\
\\
**Moon Yirah**\\
\\
10 years of experience\\
\\
\\
By The Moon is a holistic support practice dedicated to guiding women through life’s key transitions with care, intention, and compassion. As a Holistic Divinity Doula, I provide grounded, intuitive support during experiences of birth, healing, personal growth, and transformation.\\
\\
Rooted in a holistic and spiritually informed approach, my work focuses on creating a safe, supportive environment where clients feel empowered, seen, and equipped to navigate their journey with confidence.\\
\\
By The Moon is committed to supporting you every step of the way.](https://www.bornbir.com/moon-yirah)

[![Stephanie Harris](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/99trlx87twmqt2zjd2i8iv3hhkhc.png)\\
\\
Big Sandy, Texas\\
\\
**Stephanie Harris**\\
\\
11 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Stephanie, the heart behind Cedar & Honey Birth Co. I’m a birth doula and licensed massage therapist with over a decade of experience supporting moms through pregnancy, birth, and the postpartum season.\\
My approach to birth is calm, steady, and deeply supportive. I believe every mom deserves to feel informed, respected, and well cared for — physically, emotionally, and spiritually — throughout her journey. I offer hands-on comfort measures, evidence-based education, advocacy in hospital settings, and continuous presence so you feel supported and confident during birth.\\
With a background in medical and therapeutic massage, I bring advanced bodywork skills into labor support — helping with comfort, relaxation, positioning, and nervous system regulation. I’m trauma-informed, intuitive, and experienced working alongside healthcare professionals, allowing me to support you confidently whether your birth follows your hopes or takes an unexpected turn.\\
Faith is an important part of who I am, and I naturally integrate prayer, encouragement, and spiritual support into my care. This is woven into the way I serve moms and families, with gentleness, reverence, and trust in God’s presence throughout birth.\\
My heart is to create a peaceful, reassuring space where you feel prepared, supported, and confident as you welcome your baby. It would be an honor to walk this journey with you.](https://www.bornbir.com/stephanie-harris)

[![Tabitha Oogjen](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/qyny55jos5go88glolx1gep27l55.jpeg)\\
\\
Tyler, Texas\\
\\
**Tabitha Oogjen**\\
\\
4 years of experience\\
\\
\\
I created Mama Bare Doula LLC out of a passion for walking alongside mothers and families during one of the most meaningful times of their lives. My philosophy is simple: every mother deserves to feel supported, cared for, and confident from pregnancy through postpartum. I believe birth is not only about bringing a baby into the world but also about nurturing and strengthening the mother. My role is to provide comfort, advocacy, and encouragement while helping families embrace their unique journey. Whether you are preparing for birth at home, in a birth center, or in the hospital, I am committed to honoring your choices and holding space for your story while offering continuous care and heartfelt support.](https://www.bornbir.com/tabitha-oogjen)

[![Camilla Morgan](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/9z4xwjcgkz7ankszrdcdjwwzjvtl.jpeg)\\
\\
Tyler, Texas\\
\\
**Camilla Morgan**\\
\\
5 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hello, I'm Cam—a passionate birth and postpartum doula on a mission to provide unwavering support to individuals and families throughout the incredible journey of childbirth and early parenthood. My own transformative experiences as a mother ignited a deep commitment to empower and nurture others during this pivotal chapter of life, leading me to embark on the fulfilling path of doula work.\\
\\
My journey as a birth doula revolves around creating positive and empowering birthing experiences. I'm dedicated to offering emotional encouragement, evidence-based information, and physical comfort to expectant parents as they navigate the intricacies of labor and delivery. Advocating for birthing individuals, I ensure they feel informed, empowered, and supported in making choices aligned with their values and preferences. Seamlessly transitioning into my role as a postpartum doula, I extend support to new parents, providing guidance on newborn care, breastfeeding, and postpartum recovery. My non-judgmental and compassionate approach fosters a secure space, allowing families to thrive during the transformative early days of parenthood.](https://www.bornbir.com/camilla-morgan)

[![Mallory Villafuerte](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/onn3p813hyz3749moe21ss5uvdb2.jpg)\\
\\
Tyler, Texas\\
\\
**Mallory Villafuerte**\\
\\
4 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi, I’m Mallory the heart behind The Crunchy-ish Doula.\\
I’ve been married to my husband for nine years, and together we’re raising three beautiful children: a curious 7 year old, a spirited 4 year old, and our sweet 2 year old. Motherhood has been one of my greatest teachers, and every birth story I carry my own and the ones I’m honored to witness has shaped the way I serve families.\\
I welcomed my first child in a hospital setting. While I’m grateful for that experience, it also opened my eyes to how unprepared and disconnected many women can feel during birth. After that birth, and through the heartache of several miscarriages, I spent a lot of time praying asking the Lord to show me my purpose and how He wanted to use my story.\\
In His perfect timing, God answered a lifelong prayer.\\
After the home birth of my second son, everything became clear. I experienced firsthand the power of education, support, and trust in the body God designed. That birth changed me and in 2021, I became a doula.\\
My calling is to walk alongside families with open hands and a steady presence, honoring both faith and informed decision making. I believe God created our bodies with intention and wisdom, and I also believe there are moments when medical intervention is necessary and life saving. My role is never to push an agenda, but to help you prepare, discern, and advocate with confidence and peace no matter where or how you give birth.\\
Birth is sacred. Mothers are strong. And no family should feel alone in this season.\\
It would be an honor to support you.](https://www.bornbir.com/mallory-villafuerte)

[![Rebekah Blouin](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/irtgqd6ex5i9829dycg13ywhx0o1.jpeg)\\
\\
West Monroe, Louisiana\\
\\
5.0\\

(1 review)\\
\\
\\
**Rebekah Blouin**\\
\\
I am the doula behind Noble + Grace Hollow. I am a graduate of Lee University with a degree in Psychology and certified through Birthworker Academy. A wife and mother of two, I am experienced with birth in the hospital setting and also in a homebirth setting. My diverse childbirth experiences allow me to assist you in achieving the birth you always dreamed of and I focus my energy on providing mothers with the support necessary to make the transition into motherhood with strength. My passion for birth began in 2015 and has now flourished into a business that I pour my soul into to serve my clients. \\
\\
Some fun facts about me:\\
\- I am an aspiring homesteader and current backyard chicken farmer \\
\- Love being outdoors and fitness\\
\- Can never answer those quizzes that ask either sweet tea or coffee because I love both\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
Bekah was my Prenatal/Birthing Class Instructor. She also was my doula throughout my 2 day labor and outstanding support throughout my VBAC. Bekah's ability to educate and talk you through decision making is top notch. She is a great communicator, and available when she says she will be. Bekah has a true gift for helping women empower themselves through childbirth. I firmly believe I would not have had the birth experience I wanted without her by my side. My husband, Bradley, agrees. I hope to be able to have her support throughout the future! Proud to call her my doula and my friend. \\
\\
\- Jordan C.](https://www.bornbir.com/rebekah-blouin)

[![Haley Castillo](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/development/es1y2hxi6hkt8vw28qepmmjocegy.jpeg)\\
\\
Lindale, Texas\\

**Haley Castillo**\\

3 years of experience\\
\\
\\
As your doula, my goal is to support and guide you throughout your pregnancy and childbirth journey. I am dedicated to helping you make informed decisions, be\\
your own best advocate, and\\
experience a positive birth. I believe that women are powerful and\\
that birth is an empowering experience. I am here to remind you of the incredible strength you already possess, so you can achieve the birth you desire.\\
\\
I provide non-judgmental support for\\
both you and your partner, creating a\\
nurturing and safe environment.](https://www.bornbir.com/haley-castillo)

[![Courtney Handy](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/ia5fd5p6cdwf2ey55y0e55z07ydg.JPG)\\
\\
Monroe, Louisiana\\
\\
**Courtney Handy**\\
\\
11 years of experience\\
\\
\\
Hi! My name is Courtney Handy, and I am passionate about supporting families through pregnancy, birth, and postpartum. I provide compassionate, personalized care to help parents feel confident, informed, and empowered during this special journey. My goal is to offer a calm, supportive presence and help make the birth experience as positive and meaningful as possible.](https://www.bornbir.com/courtney-handy)

[![Telisse Stanfield](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/64pd37cxf8zmkzowzxzmk5i920ge.jpeg)\\

Quinlan, Texas\\

**Telisse Stanfield**\\
\\
9 years of experience\\
\\
\\
I am a wife, mother, birth, and postpartum, Doula, certified breast-feeding, counselor, newborn care, specialist, night care specialist](https://www.bornbir.com/telisse-stanfield-727)

[![Abby Chandler](https://res.cloudinary.com/bornbir/image/upload/c_thumb,f_auto,g_faces,h_200,w_200/v1/production/raejrb4ufwnxpsqm4duwyfh602sq.jpg)\\
\\
Greenville, Texas\\
\\
5.0\\
\\
(98 reviews)\\
\\
\\
**Abby Chandler**\\
\\
6 years of experience\\
\\
\\
I’ve always been drawn to birth and babies—what started as childhood curiosity grew into a calling after the birth of my first child. In 2020, I became a certified birth doula, and today I serve families across the Dallas/Fort Worth area with personalized support through pregnancy, birth, and beyond. Whether you’re planning a hospital, home, or surrogacy journey, my role is to support your choices and vision—offering practical help, emotional encouragement, and steady, grounded advocacy when it matters most.\\
\\
What makes my care unique is that I’ve experienced birth from multiple sides—I’ve given birth naturally with a midwife and have also served as a surrogate, helping bring a long-awaited baby into the arms of hopeful parents. These experiences give me a deep respect for how personal and sacred birth truly is. When I’m not supporting families, you’ll usually find me baking sourdough, doing something hands-on and “crunchy,” or creating alongside my kids—but at the heart of it all, birth work isn’t just what I do, it’s part of who I am.\\
\\
\\
Recent review\\
\\
Katie at Little Lilacs Birth Services was absolutely incredible! From the very beginning of my pregnancy, she was there to guide me through every step, patiently answering my million questions and offering expert advice that always made me feel supported and confident. She helped us revisit and refresh all the birth techniques we had learned before, giving us the tools and reassurance we needed to achieve another unmedicated birth with confidence.\\
\\
During labor and birth, Katie was calm, attentive, and completely present. Her support went beyond just guidance—she anticipated our needs, encouraged us when we felt unsure, and helped create a birth experience that felt empowering and safe.\\
\\
Beyond her professional expertise, Katie has a warmth and kindness that makes her feel like a lifelong friend. She truly cares about the families she works with and goes above and beyond to make the journey from pregnancy to birth as smooth and joyful as possible. I can’t recommend Katie and Little Lilacs Birth Services enough to anyone looking for a doula who combines knowledge, compassion, and genuine friendship.\\

## Doula Services FAQs

### What is a doula and why should you hire one?

A doula is a trained support specialist who provides continuous physical, emotional, and informational support to the mother before, during, and after childbirth. 98.6% of births in the U.S. take place in hospitals, and nearly all of them require some kind of medical intervention. However, doula-supported deliveries are linked to shorter labor, decreased need for medications, fewer c-sections, higher rates of breastfeeding initiation, less anxiety, and less postpartum depression.


### Can you pay for a doula with HSA or FSA Funds in Shreveport, LA?

To use HSA or FSA funds for doula services, they generally needed to be considered medically necessary. You might need a doctor's note or prescription stating that doula services are necessary for a specific medical condition. Keep detailed records of payments and any supporting documentation, such as a doctor's note or prescription, to substantiate that the expense was medically necessary.


### How much does a birth doula cost in Shreveport, LA?

The cost of a doula in Shreveport, LA can vary depending on the experience and services offered by the doula. On average, the cost of hiring a doula in Shreveport, LA can range from $800 to $1200. Some doulas may offer sliding scale fees or payment plans to accommodate different budgets. It's best to contact individual doulas in the area to inquire about their specific pricing and packages.


### Are doulas covered by insurance in Shreveport, LA?

Whether insurance will cover the cost of a doula in Shreveport, LA, depends on your specific insurance plan and provider. Some insurance companies may offer partial or full coverage for doula services, particularly if they are deemed medically necessary or included in a maternity care package. It's best to contact your insurance provider directly to inquire about coverage for doula services, as policies and coverage options vary widely. Additionally, some doulas may offer payment plans or sliding scale fees if insurance does not cover the cost.


### What types of doulas are for hire in Shreveport, LA?

In Shreveport, LA, you can find various types of doulas for hire, including birth doulas, postpartum doulas, and occasionally specialized doulas such as bereavement or fertility doulas. Birth doulas provide emotional, physical, and informational support during pregnancy and labor, helping expectant parents through the birthing process. Postpartum doulas focus on supporting families after the baby is born, assisting with newborn care, breastfeeding, household tasks, and emotional well-being. Some doulas may also offer additional services such as childbirth education, lactation support, or sibling care. The availability of specific types of doulas may vary, so it’s helpful to research local providers or doula organizations in the Shreveport area.


### When should I hire a doula in pregnancy?

You can start researching and contacting doulas as early as your first trimester. This allows you ample time to find a doula who aligns with your birth plan and build a rapport with them. Many people choose to hire a doula during their second trimester, around weeks 12 to 27. By this point, you likely have a clearer understanding of your birth preferences and can engage a doula who can provide support tailored to your needs. If you’re in your third trimester or even approaching your due date, it’s not too late to hire a doula. Some individuals make this decision later in pregnancy due to various reasons, and doulas can still offer valuable support and information during this time. The availability of doulas in your area may also influence when you hire one. In some regions, experienced doulas may have limited availability, so it’s wise to start your search early to secure the support you desire.


### What's the difference between a midwife and a doula?

A midwife is a trained health professional who helps women during labor, delivery, and after the birth of their babies. Midwives can provide gynecological examinations, prenatal care, and postpartum support. They are qualified to deliver babies and are trained to handle certain complications during childbirth. A doula, on the other hand, is a non-medical professional who provides emotional, physical, and educational support to a mother who is expecting, is experiencing labor, or has recently given birth. The doula's role is to help women have a safe, memorable, and empowering birthing experience. They are known for their continuous support, beginning during pregnancy and lasting through the postpartum period. While midwives have a broad scope of practice that is clinical in nature, doulas specialize in the non-clinical aspects of care. They might be preferred by mothers who are looking for continuous bedside support that is not typically provided by the medical staff. Doulas are known for their advocacy for the mother's wishes during childbirth, which can be especially valuable in hospital settings where the mother may desire a birth plan that deviates from standard procedures.


### How much does it cost to use Bornbir?

Bornbir is entirely free for new and expecting parents to use. To begin, simply tell our community of doulas what you need in your job posting and let the right providers come to you. You can then engage in direct conversations with top-rated providers to learn more and make informed decisions. Our goal is to facilitate a seamless and accessible experience for you as you embark on this transformative journey. Get started [here](https://www.bornbir.com/onboarding/intro_modal/new?mode=broadcast).
